name: Prakashatman
dates_notes: Date heavily contested: traditional 10th c. (Vidyasankar; older consensus), modern Hacker / Potter 13th c. The corpus adopts the Hacker-late dating; on this dating Prakāśātman cannot be the personal teacher of Vimuktātman or Ānandabodha, and the lineage edges to predecessors (Padmapāda, Sarvajñātman) are doctrinal-school, not personal.
core_thesis: Prakāśātman is the system-builder of *Vivaraṇa*-Advaita: the sub-school takes its name from his *Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa* and defines itself against Vāchaspati's *Bhāmatī* on every load-bearing question. The intra-school disagreement is one of formal locus, not vocabulary. Vāchaspati located *avidyā* in the *jīva* on the analysis at BSB 1.4.3 and 4.1.3; Prakāśātman, glossing the same *sūtras*, locates it in Brahman. Brahman is at once the *āśraya* and the *viṣaya* of avidyā — the *brahmāśrita-avidyā-vāda*. Avidyā is *bhāva-rūpā* (positively existent) yet *anirvacanīya* (indeterminable as either *sat* or *asat*). The *jīva* is *cidābhāsa*, a *pratibimba* of Brahman in this beginningless ignorance. Three further commitments fall out. The Mahāvākya, qua *śabda-pramāṇa*, generates an *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* whose function is to remove avidyā, though knowledge itself is never produced. *Jñāna-karma-samuccaya* is firmly rejected: *karma* purifies but cannot occasion *jñāna*. Error is analysed as *anirvacanīya-khyāti*. With Sarvajñātman's *Saṃkṣepa-Śārīraka* upstream and Vidyāraṇya's *Vivaraṇa-Prameya-Saṃgraha* downstream, Prakāśātman supplies the technical vocabulary in which most subsequent Advaita debate is conducted.

engaged_works.title: Pancapadika-Vivarana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; the school-defining text of Vivaraṇa-Advaita.
engaged_works.summary: Massive gloss-of-a-gloss on Padmapāda's *Pañcapādikā*, itself a sub-commentary on the opening five *pādas* of Śaṅkara's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*. Padmapāda's compressed positions are here converted into a fully articulated metaphysics and epistemology; a sub-school is founded in the process. The principal contributions: the *brahmāśrita-avidyā-vāda* — Brahman is both *āśraya* and *viṣaya* of avidyā, contra Vāchaspati's *jīva*-locus analysis; the *bhāva-rūpā-anirvacanīya-avidyā* doctrine — ignorance is positively existent though indeterminable as either *sat* or *asat*; the *cidābhāsa* / *pratibimba* analysis of the *jīva*; the doctrine that the Mahāvākya, qua *śabda-pramāṇa*, produces an *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* whose role is to dissolve avidyā; and a sustained refutation of *jñāna-karma-samuccaya*. The work generates the largest commentarial tradition in later Advaita: the *Vivaraṇa-Prameya-Saṃgraha* of Vidyāraṇya, the *Tātparya-Dīpikā* of Citsukha, the *Tattva-Dīpana* of Akhaṇḍānanda. It is the implicit interlocutor of nearly all post-thirteenth-century Advaita scholasticism.

key_passages.locus_long: Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa, brahmaṇo lakṣaṇopalakṣaṇe discussion, p. 212 (scan p. 269)
key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
māyāvidyayoḥ sāmānādhikaraṇyāc ca ।
tasmād anirvacanīya-māyā-viśiṣṭaṃ kāraṇaṃ brahmeti prāptam ॥

pada_analysis.morphology: nom sg neut past pass part

samasa_vigrahas.note: The dual genitive marks the two terms whose referential identity is being asserted.
Technical grammatical term for appositional co-reference.
Brahman is not reduced to māyā; it is said to be 'qualified by' māyā for causal explanation.

karaka_structure.note: Genitive relation governing sāmānādhikaraṇya.
Ablative of reason: the conclusion follows because māyā and avidyā are co-referential.
Predicate complex of the conclusion summarized by prāptam.

key_passages.english_close: And because māyā and avidyā are co-referential, it follows that Brahman is the cause as qualified by indeterminable māyā.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (roughly: the projecting/veiling power spoken of in Advaita cosmology)
(roughly: beginningless ignorance, not merely absence of information)
(roughly: not finally sayable as either real or unreal)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is a fair representative passage because it states, in the compressed technical idiom later associated with the Vivaraṇa school, the equation of māyā and avidyā and the resulting claim that causal Brahman is Brahman qualified by anirvacanīya māyā. Given the image-only state of the local scan, this is also one of the clearest recoverable loci for Prakāśātman's causal ontology in the edition on disk.
key_passages.source_edition: Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa, Digital Library of India scan (DLI item 2015.496088; E.J. Publication, Pune), transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/prakasatman_pancapadika_vivarana_archive.pdf with Archive OCR derivatives checked against the scan pagination.

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
devadatto hi svātmānam andhatvādy-upetaṃ pratibimbe 'vagacchann api tattva-jñānatvān nānuśocati ।
evaṃ brahmāpi svātmani jīve pratibimbe saṃsāraṃ paśyad api tattva-jñānatvān nānuśocati ॥

pada_analysis.gloss: endowed with blindness and the like

pada_analysis.morphology: pres act part nom sg masc
pres act part nom sg neut

samasa_vigrahas.note: The reflected self appears qualified by contingent defects.
Ablative of reason: possession of true knowledge.

karaka_structure.note: Subject of anuśocati in the first sentence.
Object of avagacchan.
Locus in which the defect-laden self is cognized.
Subject of anuśocati in the second sentence.
Layered locative: the jīva is the reflection in Brahman's own self.
Object of paśyat.

verb_modality.note: First occurrence: Devadatta does not grieve.
Second occurrence: Brahman does not grieve.

key_passages.english_close: For Devadatta, even while cognizing his own self as endowed with blindness and the like in a reflection, does not grieve, because he possesses true knowledge. In the same way, Brahman too, even while seeing saṃsāra in the reflected jīva within its own self, does not grieve, because it possesses true knowledge.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (roughly: a reflection-image, not an independent second substance)
(roughly: the cycle of bondage, transmigration, and suffering)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is representative because it makes the pratibimba doctrine do explanatory work: the jīva's bondage appears in Brahman without making Brahman itself a sufferer. That is the point of the Vivaraṇa move, not a decorative metaphor, so the passage fairly captures Prakāśātman's ontology of the individual self.
key_passages.source_edition: Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa, Digital Library of India scan (DLI item 2015.496088; E.J. Publication, Pune), transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/prakasatman_pancapadika_vivarana_archive.pdf with Archive OCR derivatives checked against the scan pagination.

key_passages.locus_long: Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa, svayaṃprakāśe 'pi mananādi-sahakārair eva mahāvākyasyāparokṣa-prayojakatvam, p. 103 (scan p. 123)
key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
svayaṃprakāśe 'pi mananādi-sahakārair eva mahāvākyasyāparokṣa-prayojakatvam ।
brahmaiva sarva-saṃvid-upādānatvād brahmakāra-śabda-pramāṇa-janya-saṃvedane 'pi brahmāpi prathamam evāparokṣatayāvabhāsate ।
tac ca cittasyātisūkṣmatvād viparyaya-saṃskāra-doṣāc ca pratibaddhaṃ bhrāntyā parokṣavad bhāsate ॥

pada_analysis.gloss: because of being the substrate/material basis of every cognition
in the cognition generated by the verbal pramāṇa taking Brahman as its form
because of the defect of contrary latent impressions

pada_analysis.morphology: nom sg neut past pass part

samasa_vigrahas.note: Used in Advaita for consciousness that does not need another revealer.
The auxiliary disciplines beginning with reflection.
The efficacy of yielding immediate realization.
The cognition produced by scriptural testimony in the form of Brahman.

karaka_structure.note: Instrumental auxiliaries needed for the mahāvākya's full efficacy.
Genitive relation with aparokṣa-prayojakatvam.
Subject of avabhāsate.
The cognitive locus in which Brahman appears.
Mode in which Brahman appears.
Subject of bhāsate: the same immediate Brahman-cognition.
Instrumental cause of appearing as if indirect.

key_passages.english_close: Even in the case of what is self-luminous, the great sentence has the capacity to produce immediacy only through auxiliaries such as reflection. Because Brahman itself is the substrate of every cognition, Brahman too appears from the outset as immediate even in the cognition generated by the verbal pramāṇa taking Brahman as its form. And that, because of the mind's extreme subtlety and because of the defect of contrary latent impressions, appears through error as if indirect.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (roughly: a 'great sentence' of śruti such as tat tvam asi)
(roughly: verbal testimony as a valid means of knowledge)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is the compact school-defining epistemological claim: the mahāvākya is genuinely liberating knowledge, but its immediacy is articulated through mananādi as sahakārin rather than through ritual action. It fairly represents Prakāśātman's position because later Vivaraṇa pedagogy and polemic repeatedly return to exactly this model of scriptural cognition.
key_passages.source_edition: Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa, Digital Library of India scan (DLI item 2015.496088; E.J. Publication, Pune), transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/prakasatman_pancapadika_vivarana_archive.pdf with Archive OCR derivatives checked against the scan pagination.

key_passages.locus_long: Pañcapādikā-Vivaraṇa, samuccaya discussion in brahma-vicāre kim-ānantaryam atha-śabdāvagamyam, pp. 164-165 (scan pp. 186-187)
key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
avidyayā mṛtyuṃ tīrtvā vidyayāmṛtam aśnuta iti phala-bhedāgamāt ।
tasmān na samuccaye pramāṇam asti ।
sarvānarthopādānānādi-mithyājñāna-nivṛttir eva vijñāne phalavad iti na samuccayāvakāśaḥ ॥

pada_analysis.gloss: because scripture teaches a difference of fruits
the cessation of beginningless false cognition that is the substrate of all misfortune

samasa_vigrahas.note: Ablative of reason grounded in scriptural teaching.

samasa_vigrahas.resolution: sarvānarthānām upādānaṃ yad anādi mithyājñānaṃ tasya nivṛttiḥ
samasa_vigrahas.note: The causal root of bondage is beginningless false cognition.

karaka_structure.note: Instrument with tīrtvā in the cited scriptural half-verse.
Object crossed.
Instrument by which immortality is attained.
Object attained.
Subject of asti in the doctrinal conclusion.
Locative domain: the proposed combination of knowledge and karma.
Predicate subject in the final nominal conclusion.
Locus in which this cessation appears as the fruit.

key_passages.english_close: From the scriptural teaching 'having crossed death by avidyā, one attains immortality by vidyā,' because the fruits are distinct, there is no valid warrant for a combination. Since in liberating knowledge the fruit is precisely the cessation of beginningless false cognition, the substrate of all misfortune, there is no room for any such combination.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (roughly: liberating knowledge, here contrasted with karmic or lower disciplines)
(roughly: a co-functioning combination of two means)
(roughly: realized knowledge, not mere conceptual awareness)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is a representative anti-samuccaya passage because it argues from scriptural citation and from the differing causal roles of karma and vijñāna, not from polemical shorthand. It fairly captures Prakāśātman's position that karma may purify the aspirant, but liberating knowledge alone removes the beginningless root of bondage.
